data.content: I'll start `dotnet watch test` which will automatically re-run tests whenever you save a file.

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

data.reasoningText: The user has a .NET test project using MSTest. They want hot reload via `dotnet watch test` so tests re-run automatically on file changes. Let me start `dotnet watch test` for them.

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-36190312fd2c44e7a5861cd4d1f8b6ed && dotnet watch test --no-hot-reload 2>&1", "description": "Start dotnet watch test for hot reload", "mode": "async", "shellId": "watch"}

data.content: `dotnet watch` is running. There's one build error — a missing `using System;`. Let me fix that so the tests compile:
data.reasoningText: There's a build error - `ArgumentException` needs `using System;`. Let me fix that and watch it automatically re-run.

data.content:
`dotnet watch test` is up and running. It detected the fix, rebuilt, and ran the tests. Here's the current status:

- ✅ `CalculateShipping_StandardDelivery_Returns5` — passed
- ❌ `CalculateShipping_ExpressDelivery_Returns15` — failed (expected `15.00`, got `10.00`)

The `"express"` case in `CalculateShipping` returns `10.00m` but the test expects `15.00m`. The watcher is live — just edit `ShippingCalculatorTests.cs` and it will automatically rebuild and re-run the tests on save.
